entries.d: To create something completely new that didn't exist before
entries.s: Who first _____ the telephone back in 1876? It was Alexander Graham Bell — no one had created one before.

entries.d: To try new things to find out what happens
entries.s: Scientists in their white lab coats _____ in their clean labs with chemicals to discover brand new things about the world.

entries.d: To make something better than before
entries.s: She _____ her English speaking skills a lot over one year by watching English films without subtitles every single day.

entries.d: To find something or learn about something for the first time
entries.s: The curious children _____ a secret hidden path in the forest that nobody in the village had ever seen before.

entries.d: To find the answer by thinking about it
entries.s: I can't _____ the correct answer to question five in the maths test — the numbers don't add up no matter what I try.

entries.d: To make a plan or drawing for how something will look
entries.s: She loves fashion and wants to _____ her own unique collection of stylish clothes one day with her own logo and brand.

entries.d: To test something to see how it works or if you like it
entries.s: Let's _____ this new chocolate cake recipe from the cookbook today — it looks delicious and sounds easy to make.

entries.d: To make or create something in large amounts
entries.s: This big factory in China _____ more than one million colourful plastic toys every single year for children around the world.

entries.d: Thin slices of salted or smoked meat from a pig
entries.s: I like to have scrambled eggs and crispy fried _____ — thin strips of pork meat — for breakfast on lazy Sunday mornings.

entries.d: To make something look more beautiful by adding things to it
entries.s: We _____ the classroom brightly with colourful balloons, paper chains, and streamers for the birthday party this afternoon.

entries.d: Food that has been prepared in a particular way
entries.s: My absolute favourite _____ is pasta with homemade tomato sauce — my grandmother's special recipe that she taught my mum.

entries.d: The oily substance in food or under the skin
entries.s: Too much unhealthy _____ in fast food like burgers and fries is really not good for your health and can make you sick.

entries.d: Something new that someone has created
entries.s: The electric light bulb in 1879 was an amazing _____ by Thomas Edison that completely changed the world at night.

entries.d: A large group of people in one place
entries.s: A big noisy _____ of about 500 people waited outside the cinema for two hours for the new Marvel film to start.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with CROWD?

entries.d: The flow of electricity through a wire
entries.s: Be very careful — there is a strong electric _____ flowing through these exposed wires and it could give you a bad shock.

entries.d: To create something new over a period of time
entries.s: The clever software team _____ a new mobile app for learning Spanish and French over the past six months.

entries.d: Using or producing electricity
entries.s: My dad wants to buy a new modern car with a quiet _____ motor that uses batteries instead of petrol.

entries.d: Power that makes machines work or keeps things moving
entries.s: Wind turbines and solar panels can give us clean renewable _____ without polluting the environment or using fossil fuels.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with ENERGY?

entries.d: The power to change what other people think or do
entries.s: Good kind teachers have a big positive _____ on their students' lives and can shape who they become as adults.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with INFLUENCE?

entries.d: A person who creates something new
entries.s: She wants to be a creative _____ and make new helpful machines that solve everyday problems for people around the world.

entries.d: To spend money on something hoping to get more back
entries.s: He _____ all his savings of €5000 in a new small coffee shop business that opened last year in his town.

entries.d: Maybe, possibly
entries.s: _____ we can go to the cinema this weekend — maybe on Saturday afternoon, but I'm not completely sure yet.

entries.d: To be the one who caused something or must take care of it
entries.s: Who is _____ for cleaning the whole classroom today after the last lesson — it needs sweeping and wiping.

entries.d: To send something out fast, like a ball or an arrow
entries.s: He _____ the football directly into the goal from ten metres away and his team scored the winning point.

entries.d: Feeling sure about yourself and your abilities
entries.s: She felt _____ about the big maths test because she had studied very hard for it for two whole weeks.

entries.d: To make someone think you are very good at something
entries.s: He _____ all his friends at the birthday party with an amazing magic card trick that nobody could figure out.

entries.d: Something you use with water to wash your hands and body
entries.s: Always use _____ and warm water when you wash your dirty hands to kill the germs and keep yourself healthy.

entries.d: A piece of equipment made for a special purpose
entries.s: A smartphone is a very useful electronic _____ — a small portable machine with a screen that you carry everywhere in your pocket.

entries.d: Something that is made to be sold
entries.s: This supermarket sells only _____ that are made in Austria — milk, cheese, honey, and bread all from local farmers.

entries.d: Very special and worth noticing
entries.s: She gave a _____ five-minute speech about saving the environment that impressed everyone in the audience with clever ideas.

entries.d: To study something carefully to learn more about it
entries.s: The students _____ the topic of climate change online for two hours before writing their homework essays for science class.

entries.d: The part of your body between your hand and your arm
entries.s: She wears a beautiful silver bracelet on her left _____ — the joint between her hand and her arm.

entries.d: Sticks you put under your arms to help you walk when your leg is hurt
entries.s: He had to use wooden _____ under his arms to walk around for three weeks after he broke his foot playing football.

entries.d: A health problem that makes you feel unwell
entries.s: She missed a lot of school because of a long serious _____ that kept her in hospital for two months.

entries.d: A slope that connects two different levels
entries.s: There is a smooth _____ at the entrance of the school instead of steps, so people in wheelchairs can easily get inside.

entries.d: A chair with wheels for people who cannot walk
entries.s: The injured boy uses a silver _____ with four wheels to move around the school corridors while his broken leg heals.

entries.d: To change something or yourself to fit a new situation
entries.s: She quickly _____ to her new school in a different country and made lots of new friends within the first two weeks.

entries.d: To join or fix one thing to another
entries.s: I accidentally _____ the wrong file to my important email to the teacher — I sent my music playlist instead of my homework.

entries.d: A piece of clothing you wear on your hand to keep it warm
entries.s: It's freezing cold outside with snow on the ground — don't forget your warm woolly _____ for your hands!

entries.d: The work you do to keep a house clean, like washing and cooking
entries.s: Everyone in our family of four has to help with the _____ — washing dishes, vacuuming, and taking out the bins on Sundays.

entries.d: Working by itself, without a person doing it
entries.s: The sliding glass doors of the big supermarket open _____ when you walk near them without you having to push or touch anything.

entries.d: The part of a shirt around the neck, or a band around an animal's neck
entries.s: The big brown dog has a red leather _____ around its neck with its name 'Rex' and owner's phone number.

entries.d: The study of how computers work and how to program them
entries.s: She is very interested in _____ and wants to learn computer programming and coding in Python this year.

entries.d: A person who designs or builds machines, roads or bridges
entries.s: The clever _____ designed and built a small helpful robot that can vacuum and clean floors by itself in 30 minutes.

entries.d: A feeling or idea that makes you want to create or do something
entries.s: Beautiful nature is often a great _____ for famous artists who paint landscapes, trees, flowers, and mountains.

entries.d: To fix something that is broken
entries.s: The experienced mechanic said he could _____ the broken washing machine with new parts and make it work again like new.

entries.d: To help someone or give them what they need
entries.s: My kind parents always _____ me emotionally when I have a problem at school — they listen and give good advice.
